variant: quick heads up: i'm vegetarian and i work out at 6am before work

request.message: quick heads up: i'm vegetarian and i work out at 6am before work

response_body.response: got it, early morning lifter on a plant-based diet—that's actually solid for consistency. we'll need to dial in your protein timing around those workouts, but it's totally doable.

variant: what should i eat after my workout?

request.message: what should i eat after my workout?

response_body.response: post-workout you want protein + carbs within an hour or two of finishing. that refills glycogen, speeds recovery, and keeps your muscles full. since you're vegetarian and training at 6am, aim for at least 30g protein in that meal. good options: greek yogurt + granola + berries, cottage cheese + toast + jam, tofu scramble with rice, lentil pasta with olive oil and veggies, or a pea protein shake with oats and banana. carbs matter just as much as protein post-workout—they drive pumps and refill what you burned in the session. rice, potatoes, oats, fruit, or toast all work. pair them with your protein source and you're set.

products.name: Optimum Nutrition Gold Standard 100% Whey Protein
products.description: 24g whey isolate per scoop — third-party tested, low-cost per gram.

variant: and when should i eat it?

request.message: and when should i eat it?

response_body.response:
timing-wise, eat within 1-2 hours after your 6am workout ends. since you're done early, that's probably around 7-8am, which works perfectly before work. if you can't eat solid food right after (rushing out), a shake with pea protein powder, oats, and banana takes 2 minutes and hits the same targets.

the window matters more than the exact minute—anything in that 1-2 hour range does the job. if you're starving immediately post-workout, a quick shake right after training, then a bigger meal an hour later, works too.
